skill.name: google-news-api-skill
skill.description: Extract structured Google News results through the BrowserAct API using a Python helper script. The skill supports keyword searches, time filters, and result limits for news monitoring and research workflows.
skill.summary: Search Google News through BrowserAct and return structured article results.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static findings for weak cryptography, PowerShell execution, reconnaissance, and most markdown backticks are false positives from comments or documentation. The skill does perform authenticated network calls to BrowserAct, and it instructs agents to ask users for an API key in chat, creating a high credential exposure risk. No evidence found of malicious obfuscation, unauthorized exfiltration, or prompt injection in the reviewed files.

high_findings.title: Credential Disclosure Encouraged in Chat
high_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill tells the agent to ask and wait for the user to provide BROWSERACT_API_KEY when it is missing, and the script message also says the key may be provided in chat. This can expose a long-lived API credential to conversation logs or unintended tool context. Use an environment variable or secret manager instead.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact user-facing instructions direct API key disclosure through chat when the environment variable is absent. This is a clear credential handling risk even though no malicious endpoint is present.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated External API Calls
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY and sends it as a Bearer token to the BrowserAct workflow API. This appears aligned with the skill purpose, but users should understand that keywords and API credentials are sent to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls and Authorization header are explicit and semantically necessary for BrowserAct. The risk is disclosure to a third-party API, not hidden exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: External Script Execution Required
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE WITH LIMITED SCOPE: The documentation instructs agents to run the bundled Python script. The command uses a local script and quoted user arguments, so it is expected behavior for this skill, but it still expands execution privileges beyond pure prompting.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The required command is explicit and local, with no shell metacharacter construction in the Python file. Risk remains because marketplace users must permit script execution.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives Dismissed
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The PowerShell finding is a comment about UTF-8 compatibility, markdown backtick findings are mostly inline formatting or a documented command, and weak cryptography or reconnaissance intent was not evidenced in the reviewed lines. No evidence found of prompt injection text, encoded payloads, or hidden command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited contexts shows documentation, comments, or string matching guidance rather than cryptography, reconnaissance, or arbitrary command execution.

low_findings.title: Result Retrieval Control Flow Bug
low_findings.description: NEEDS_REVIEW: After the polling loop, the script always prints a timeout message and returns before the result retrieval block. This is not a security issue, but it can prevent successful output and cause repeated external API calls during retries.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The break exits the loop but execution then reaches the unconditional timeout return. The unreachable retrieval code is visible immediately after that return.

dangerous_patterns.title: Secret Requested Outside Secret Storage
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill should not ask users to paste BROWSERACT_API_KEY into chat. Secrets should be set through environment variables or a managed secret store before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Both the skill instructions and script error guidance explicitly permit sharing the API key in chat. This is a direct dangerous pattern for credential handling.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Network Submission
dangerous_patterns.description: Search keywords, date filters, and result limits are submitted to the BrowserAct API. This is required functionality but should be disclosed to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The payload and requests calls clearly send user search terms and task identifiers to BrowserAct endpoints. This is expected but privacy relevant.

content.value_statement: News monitoring often requires repeated searches and structured cleanup. This skill runs a BrowserAct workflow to collect Google News headlines, sources, links, and publish times.
actual_capabilities: Runs a local Python helper for BrowserAct Google News workflows.
Accepts search keywords from the user prompt or command line.
Supports publish date filters such as past 24 hours or past week.
Limits the requested number of returned news items.
Polls BrowserAct task status and prints progress timestamps.
Returns available headlines, sources, article links, publish times, and authors.
limitations: Requires a valid BROWSERACT_API_KEY before use.
Sends search terms and task data to the BrowserAct service.
Depends on BrowserAct availability and Google News workflow behavior.
The current script has a result retrieval bug after polling completes.

use_cases.title: Monitor brand mentions
use_cases.description: Track recent Google News coverage for a company, product, or executive.

use_cases.title: Research market movement
use_cases.description: Collect headlines and sources about a sector to support trend research.

use_cases.title: Watch competitor activity
use_cases.description: Find recent news about competitor launches, partnerships, funding, or campaigns.

prompt_templates.title: Find recent headlines
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner search for a single topic.
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Google News for recent articles about renewable energy from the past week. Limit results to 10 items.

prompt_templates.title: Monitor a brand
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Google News skill to find articles about Acme Robotics from the past 24 hours. Summarize the main sources and themes.

prompt_templates.title: Compare competitors
prompt_templates.prompt: Collect Google News results for three cloud database competitors from the past week. Group results by company and highlight product news.

prompt_templates.title: Build a research brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced topic scan with filtering and synthesis.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find up to 30 Google News items about AI regulation from the past week. Identify recurring regions, named organizations, and policy themes.

output_examples.input: Search for electric vehicle charging news from the past 24 hours.
output: A short list of recent headlines with publisher names and article links.
A brief summary of repeated topics, such as infrastructure funding or charging standards.
Publication times when available from the BrowserAct result.

output_examples.input: Monitor news about a public company for the past week.
output: Grouped coverage by source and topic.
Links to the original articles for follow-up reading.
A note when results appear sparse or unrelated.

best_practices: Set BROWSERACT_API_KEY through environment configuration, not chat messages.
Use narrow keywords and date filters to reduce noisy results.
Verify important claims against the linked source articles.
anti_patterns: Do not paste API keys into chat or shared transcripts.
Do not treat headline lists as full article analysis.
Do not run broad searches when a targeted query would reduce irrelevant results.

faq.question: Does this skill use the official Google News API?
faq.answer: No. It uses a BrowserAct workflow that extracts results from Google News.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: A BrowserAct API key is required and should be provided through BROWSERACT_API_KEY.

faq.question: Can it summarize full articles?
faq.answer: It primarily returns structured search results. Full article analysis requires opening and reviewing the linked pages.

faq.question: Can I choose a date range?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports filters such as past hours, past 24 hours, past week, and past year.

faq.question: Does it work without network access?
faq.answer: No. It requires network access to the BrowserAct API and BrowserAct access to Google News.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish as submitted?
faq.answer: No. The audit recommends fixing API key handling before publication.
